# Feldman-Cousins Intervals

The Feldman-Cousins construction matters most when standard recipes become unstable:
near physical boundaries, at low counts, and in regimes where the procedure should
switch consistently between an upper limit and a two-sided interval.

At this stage the chapter mainly fixes the ordering principle and the idea of a
unified construction. A full implementation usually requires toy Monte Carlo or a
careful tabulation.

::: {.notebook-card}
### Companion notebook

The placeholder notebook keeps a minimal table of observed counts and a draft
numerical scaffold that can later be replaced by a full construction.

<div class="notebook-actions">
  <a class="notebook-link notebook-link-primary" href="../notebooks/feldman_cousins_demo.qmd">Open notebook</a>
</div>
:::
